title: Mining Sirdar (Coalfields CBT) — Mock 01 (Free)
pattern: Coalfield Sirdar/Overman CBT (100 Q · 100 marks · 120 min · no −ve)

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A man walks 3 km North, then 4 km East. How far is he from the start?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resultant $=\sqrt{3^2+4^2}$.

**Working.** $\sqrt{9+16}=\sqrt{25}=5$ km.

**Answer.** 5 km.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the word OPPOSITE in meaning to 'ASCEND':
questions.solution: **Answer.** The opposite of 'ascend' (go up) is 'descend' (go down).

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A vehicle travels at 50 km/h for 4 hours. Distance covered is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Distance $=$ speed $\times$ time.

**Working.** $50\times 4=200$ km.

**Answer.** $200$ km.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Simple interest on ₹5000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $SI=\dfrac{P\,R\,T}{100}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{5000\times 8\times 2}{100}=800$.

**Answer.** ₹$800$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the word most similar in meaning to 'CURTAIL':
questions.solution: **Answer.** 'Curtail' means to cut short or reduce.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A vehicle travels at 60 km/h for 2.5 hours. Distance covered is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Distance $=$ speed $\times$ time.

**Working.** $60\times 2.5=150$ km.

**Answer.** $150$ km.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A sum of ₹300 is divided between two persons in the ratio 4:1. The larger share is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share $=\dfrac{\text{part}}{\text{sum of parts}}\times\text{total}$.

**Working.** Parts $=4+1=5$; larger share $=\dfrac{4}{5}\times 300=240$.

**Answer.** ₹$240$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Simple interest on ₹4000 at 10% per annum for 1.5 years is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $SI=\dfrac{P\,R\,T}{100}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{4000\times 10\times 1.5}{100}=600$.

**Answer.** ₹$600$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Coal India Limited (CIL) is headquartered in:
questions.solution: **Answer.** CIL's registered office is in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A sum of ₹320 is divided between two persons in the ratio 5:3. The larger share is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share $=\dfrac{\text{part}}{\text{sum of parts}}\times\text{total}$.

**Working.** Parts $=5+3=8$; larger share $=\dfrac{5}{8}\times 320=200$.

**Answer.** ₹$200$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: A vehicle travels at 45 km/h for 3 hours. Distance covered is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Distance $=$ speed $\times$ time.

**Working.** $45\times 3=135$ km.

**Answer.** $135$ km.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Simple interest on ₹12000 at 5% per annum for 2 years is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $SI=\dfrac{P\,R\,T}{100}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{12000\times 5\times 2}{100}=1200$.

**Answer.** ₹$1200$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 12, 18, 24, 30, 36 is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum ÷ count.

**Working.** Sum $=120$, count $=5$, so average $=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 15, 25, 35, 45, 55 is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum ÷ count.

**Working.** Sum $=175$, count $=5$, so average $=35$.

**Answer.** $35$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 10, 14, 18, 22, 26 is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum ÷ count.

**Working.** Sum $=90$, count $=5$, so average $=18$.

**Answer.** $18$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Which Indian state has the largest coal reserves?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Jharkhand holds the largest coal reserves in India, followed by Odisha and Chhattisgarh.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The capital of Rajasthan is:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Jaipur is the capital of Rajasthan.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ___
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Differences are 4, 6, 8, 10, … (increasing by 2).

**Working.** Next difference $=12$, so $30+12=42$.

**Answer.** 42.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of 8, 16, 24, 32, 40 is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum ÷ count.

**Working.** Sum $=120$, count $=5$, so average $=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Awareness & Aptitude
questions.stem: DGMS (Directorate General of Mines Safety) functions under which Union Ministry?
questions.solution: **Answer.** DGMS is under the Ministry of Labour and Employment, Government of India.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Settling (sedimentation) ponds at a mine are provided to:
options: Remove suspended solids before water is discharged
questions.solution: **Answer.** Settling ponds let suspended solids settle so discharged water meets effluent norms.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Which of the following coals has the highest fixed-carbon content and calorific value?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Coal rank rises peat → lignite → bituminous → anthracite; anthracite has the highest carbon.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A concentrator treats 2500 t of ore assaying 1.2% metal at 86% recovery. The mass of metal recovered is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal recovered $=$ feed $\times\dfrac{\text{grade}}{100}\times\dfrac{\text{recovery}}{100}$.

**Working.** $2500\times0.012\times0.86=25.8$ t.

**Answer.** $25.8$ t.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.45 m/min for 36 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.45\times36=16.2$ m.

**Answer.** $16.2$ m.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A deposit holds 20 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 2.5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{20}{2.5}=8$ years.

**Answer.** $8$ years.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.7 m³/min into a roadway carrying 40 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.7}{40}\times100=1.75\%$.

**Answer.** $1.75\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 150 t at 0.9% and 350 t at 1.8%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Weighted grade $=\dfrac{\sum(t_i g_i)}{\sum t_i}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{150\times0.9+350\times1.8}{500}=1.53\%$.

**Answer.** $1.53\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An SDL (Side Discharge Loader) in underground coal mines is used to:
options: Gather and load broken coal onto cars/conveyor
questions.solution: **Answer.** The SDL gathers blasted coal and side-discharges it to tubs or a conveyor.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Since the 2015 amendment, mineral concessions for notified minerals are granted primarily through:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The 2015 amendment introduced grant of mineral concessions by auction to ensure transparency.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A deposit holds 36 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 3 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{36}{3}=12$ years.

**Answer.** $12$ years.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Certificates of Competency (Manager, Overman, Sirdar, Surveyor) are granted by the:
options: Board of Mining Examinations under DGMS
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Board of Mining Examinations, under the Director General of Mines Safety, conducts competency examinations and grants certificates.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 55 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 1.9 m/s. Its capacity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capacity $=3.6\times(\text{kg/m})\times v\,(\text{m/s})$ t/h.

**Working.** $3.6\times55\times1.9=376.2$ t/h.

**Answer.** $376.2$ t/h.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Before firing a shot in a gassy coal seam, the shot-firer must test for inflammable gas using a:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Gas testing before shotfiring uses a flame safety lamp or approved methanometer.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.2 m³/min into a roadway carrying 25 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.2}{25}\times100=0.8\%$.

**Answer.** $0.8\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A shovel with a 12 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.9 and a cycle time of 45 s. Its theoretical output is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=\dfrac{\text{bucket}\times\text{fill factor}\times3600}{\text{cycle (s)}}$ m³/h.

**Working.** $\dfrac{12\times0.9\times3600}{45}=864$ m³/h.

**Answer.** $864$ m³/h.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Under the Mines Act, 1952, no person below the age of ___ years shall be employed in any mine or part thereof:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Section 40 prohibits employment of persons below 18 in a mine.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.4 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.4}{50}\times100=0.8\%$.

**Answer.** $0.8\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 8 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=3.5\times 8=28$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $28$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=16-0.65Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $16-0.65Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{16}{1.15}\Rightarrow Q=3.73$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $3.73$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A seam of extraction thickness 2.8 m is worked by caving with a subsidence factor of 0.6. The maximum surface subsidence is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum subsidence $S_{max}=a\times m$ (subsidence factor $\times$ extracted thickness).

**Working.** $0.6\times2.8=1.68$ m.

**Answer.** $1.68$ m.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.3 m³/min into a roadway carrying 30 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.3}{30}\times100=1\%$.

**Answer.** $1\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.3 m³/min into a roadway carrying 25 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.3}{25}\times100=1.2\%$.

**Answer.** $1.2\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: In a below-ground coal mine, smoking materials and naked lights are:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Smoking materials/naked lights are contraband below ground to prevent firedamp/coal-dust ignition.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 10000 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 30. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.025, the rope pull is approximately _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rope pull $T=Wg\left(\frac{1}{n}+\mu\right)$ (gravity + resistance).

**Working.** $T=10000\times9.81\times(\frac{1}{30}+0.025)=5722.5$ N.

**Answer.** $5722.5$ N.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: 'Stemming' placed in the collar of a blasthole serves to:
options: Confine the explosion gases and improve energy use
questions.solution: **Answer.** Inert stemming confines gases, reducing blow-out and improving fragmentation.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.15 m³/min into a roadway carrying 40 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.15}{40}\times100=0.38\%$.

**Answer.** $0.38\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Cut-and-fill stoping is most appropriate when:
options: Ore is valuable and wall rock is weak
The deposit is flat and shallow
The orebody is a thin coal seam
questions.solution: **Answer.** Cut-and-fill suits high-value ore in weak ground, where fill provides support and a working floor.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Safety in metalliferous (non-coal) mines is regulated by the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961 cover non-coal mines.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A blast detonates 100 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 100 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{100}{\sqrt{100}}=\dfrac{100}{10}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Bieniawski's RMR (Rock Mass Rating) does NOT directly include which parameter?
questions.solution: **Answer.** RMR uses UCS, RQD, discontinuity spacing/condition, groundwater and orientation adjustment — not in-situ stress directly.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 15 m² at a mean velocity of 2.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=2.5\times 15=37.5$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $37.5$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 10 m² at a mean velocity of 1.8 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.8\times 10=18$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $18$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A shovel with a 9 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.88 and a cycle time of 36 s. Its theoretical output is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=\dfrac{\text{bucket}\times\text{fill factor}\times3600}{\text{cycle (s)}}$ m³/h.

**Working.** $\dfrac{9\times0.88\times3600}{36}=792$ m³/h.

**Answer.** $792$ m³/h.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Highwall mining recovers coal from:
options: The exposed seam in the final highwall of an opencast pit
questions.solution: **Answer.** Highwall mining drives unmanned entries into the seam exposed in the opencast highwall.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A drill cut for tunnels/drivages using parallel holes with one or more uncharged relief holes is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The burn (parallel-hole) cut uses empty relief holes to create a free face for the charged holes.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Occupational noise exposure in mines is measured in:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Noise is measured in A-weighted decibels, dB(A); 90 dB(A) over 8 h is a common limit.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The poisonous gas mixture remaining after an explosion or fire, rich in carbon monoxide, is called:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Afterdamp is the post-explosion mixture; firedamp = methane, stinkdamp = H₂S, whitedamp = CO.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 4 m² at a mean velocity of 1.2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.2\times 4=4.8$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $4.8$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 7 m, spacing 5 m, bench height 11 m and rock density 2.6 t/m³. If 192.5 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=7\times5\times11=385$ m³; mass $=385\times2.6=1001$ t; PF $=\dfrac{1001}{192.5}=5.2$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $5.2$ t/kg.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A dump truck of 60 t payload completes a 18-minute haul cycle over a 450-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{450}{18}=25$; output $=25\times60=1500$ t.

**Answer.** $1500$ t.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 20 m² at a mean velocity of 2.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=2.5\times 20=50$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $50$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: In a blast round the 'burden' is the distance from a blasthole to the:
options: Nearest free face, measured perpendicular to the hole
Adjacent hole in the same row
questions.solution: **Answer.** Burden is the perpendicular distance to the nearest free face; spacing is between holes in a row.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 20 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.1\times 20^2=40$ Pa.

**Answer.** $40$ Pa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A stope yields 250 t of ore mixed with 25 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{25}{275}\times100=9.09\%$.

**Answer.** $9.09\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The gradient most favourable for self-acting (gravity) haulage of loaded tubs is one that:
options: Falls in the direction of the loaded run
questions.solution: **Answer.** In self-acting haulage the loaded tubs descend, hauling the empties up via gravity.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 7 m and height 3.5 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.2 m³/min into a roadway carrying 40 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.2}{40}\times100=0.5\%$.

**Answer.** $0.5\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.5 m³/min into a roadway carrying 15 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.5}{15}\times100=3.33\%$.

**Answer.** $3.33\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 10 m² at a mean velocity of 4 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=4\times 10=40$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $40$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 7500 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 20. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.025, the rope pull is approximately _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rope pull $T=Wg\left(\frac{1}{n}+\mu\right)$ (gravity + resistance).

**Working.** $T=7500\times9.81\times(\frac{1}{20}+0.025)=5518.13$ N.

**Answer.** $5518.13$ N.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.7 m³/min into a roadway carrying 25 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.7}{25}\times100=2.8\%$.

**Answer.** $2.8\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The gas extracted from coal seams as 'coalbed methane' (CBM) is predominantly:
questions.solution: **Answer.** CBM is mainly methane adsorbed on coal, drained to improve safety and as an energy resource.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 20 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=1\times 20^2=400$ Pa.

**Answer.** $400$ Pa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A roof bolt primarily stabilises strata by:
options: Adding dead weight to the roof
questions.solution: **Answer.** Roof bolts knit weak beds to a stronger bed (suspension/beam-building), improving self-support.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.6 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.6}{50}\times100=1.2\%$.

**Answer.** $1.2\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Before opening a major mine, the statutory study assessing environmental consequences is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** EIA, leading to an Environment Management Plan (EMP), is required for environmental clearance.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 18 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=3.5\times 18=63$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $63$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.04 m³/s of water against a head of 200 m at 70% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.04\times200}{0.7}\div1000=112.11$ kW.

**Answer.** $112.11$ kW.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1.5 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 40 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=1.5\times 40^2=2400$ Pa.

**Answer.** $2400$ Pa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Statutory mine survey plans must be kept up to date and corrected at intervals not exceeding:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Survey plans must be brought up to date at least once every three months.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Acid mine drainage (AMD) is produced mainly by the oxidation of:
options: Pyrite (iron sulphide) in the presence of water and air
questions.solution: **Answer.** Pyrite (FeS₂) oxidises to sulphuric acid and iron salts, lowering water pH.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 4 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.5\times 4=6$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $6$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=18-0.6Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $18-0.6Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{18}{1.1}\Rightarrow Q=4.05$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $4.05$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.3 m³/min into a roadway carrying 15 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.3}{15}\times100=2\%$.

**Answer.** $2\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: India's principal rock-phosphate deposit is mined at Jhamarkotra in which Rajasthan district?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Jhamarkotra (Udaipur) is India's largest rock-phosphate source, mined by RSMML.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 9 m and height 3 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=3$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times3)=10.11$ MPa.

**Answer.** $10.11$ MPa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The Rampura–Agucha mine in Rajasthan is India's largest producer of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Rampura–Agucha (Bhilwara, Rajasthan) is India's largest lead–zinc mine, operated by Hindustan Zinc.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.4 m/min for 30 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.4\times30=12$ m.

**Answer.** $12$ m.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Ventilation of a blind heading (dead-end drivage) is best achieved by:
options: Auxiliary ventilation with a fan and duct
questions.solution: **Answer.** Blind headings have no through-flow, so a forcing/exhausting auxiliary fan-and-duct is used.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 40 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=1\times 40^2=1600$ Pa.

**Answer.** $1600$ Pa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 15 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=1\times 15^2=225$ Pa.

**Answer.** $225$ Pa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 20 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.5\times 20=30$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $30$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: In metalliferous mines, the statutory first-line supervisor equivalent to the coal-mine Sirdar is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Mining Mate is the first-line supervisor under the MMR 1961.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 80 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 1.8 m/s. Its capacity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capacity $=3.6\times(\text{kg/m})\times v\,(\text{m/s})$ t/h.

**Working.** $3.6\times80\times1.8=518.4$ t/h.

**Answer.** $518.4$ t/h.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Zawar in Rajasthan is historically famous for ancient mining and smelting of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Zawar (Udaipur) has evidence of ancient zinc smelting and is a major lead–zinc field.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A shovel with a 4 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.8 and a cycle time of 20 s. Its theoretical output is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=\dfrac{\text{bucket}\times\text{fill factor}\times3600}{\text{cycle (s)}}$ m³/h.

**Working.** $\dfrac{4\times0.8\times3600}{20}=576$ m³/h.

**Answer.** $576$ m³/h.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: The chief airborne pollutant from drilling, blasting and haulage in opencast mines is:
questions.solution: **Answer.** SPM/respirable dust is the dominant opencast air pollutant; water spraying suppresses it.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 200 t at 1.5% and 300 t at 2.5%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Weighted grade $=\dfrac{\sum(t_i g_i)}{\sum t_i}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{200\times1.5+300\times2.5}{500}=2.1\%$.

**Answer.** $2.1\%$.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.4 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 10 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.4\times 10^2=40$ Pa.

**Answer.** $40$ Pa.

questions.subject: Technical (Mining)
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 6500 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 18. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.022, the rope pull is approximately _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rope pull $T=Wg\left(\frac{1}{n}+\mu\right)$ (gravity + resistance).

**Working.** $T=6500\times9.81\times(\frac{1}{18}+0.022)=4945.33$ N.

**Answer.** $4945.33$ N.
